atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence makes block-verification precompile address imports stable by re-exporting canonical async precompile constants from `ritual-precompile-addresses` through `crate::constants::*`.

destinations.evidence: Destination `constants.rs` preserves the exact precompile constant re-export list from the source-added file.
Destination verifier imports and tests use the preserved constants through the block-verification constants module.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination keeps the same canonical address re-export surface, so verifier code uses the same precompile address values as the source divergence.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Removing or changing any re-export should break verifier imports or make two-phase precompile classification tests fail.
atoms.notes: Exact mapping; no review queue entry required.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +15,28 @@; TEE registry and SPC/public-key verifier limits
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence defines verifier-local defaults and limits: default TEEServiceRegistry contract address, SPC proof signature minimum length of 65 bytes, and maximum ExecutorRegistry public key length of 128 bytes.

destinations.evidence: Destination preserves the exact default TEE registry address and numeric SPC/public-key limits from the source-added file.
Destination verifier uses the default TEE registry address in verifier configuration and uses the SPC signature length in proof-length validation and tests.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves the verifier constants and bounded uses exactly, keeping registry defaults and SPC/public-key limits aligned with the source divergence.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: SPC proof length 64 should fail while length 65 should pass the length guard; verifier config with no TEE override should use the default registry address.
atoms.notes: Exact mapping; no review queue entry required.
